specific_location: Teller Farm north trailhead, Valmont Road 0.6 mile west of 95th Street 40.04 deg N, 105.14 deg W
description: Among the birds in close proximity, the Golden-Crowned Sparrow was smaller than an American Robin and of comparable size to a Harris's Sparrow and several White-Crowned Sparrows. The Golden-Crowned Sparrow was more plump than the other zonotrichia. Most notably, it had a yellow forecrown outlined in black. The thick black band behind the eye was nearly complete, and thus the bird was nearly in breeding plumage. Otherwise, it had dull gray undersides, tan flanks, a checkered mantle, and multi-colored wings with white-tipped coverts.Behavior: The Golden-Crowned Sparrow was foraging in weeds together with other zonotrichia: one Harris's Sparrow and several White-Crowned Sparrows.Call: none heardPlumage: nearly in breeding plumage
